cases.name: Quinteros v. Harbor Distributing, LLC
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Opposition repeatedly relied on two cited cases that the court determined do not exist (non‑existent citations). Outcome: Monetary Sanction; Bar referral.

cases.name: H. C. v. Contreras
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited and the trial court relied on a nonexistent case, reproducing the language verbatim from the brief into the ruling. Outcome: Judgment reversed and remanded.

cases.name: Lagac v. Capital One, N.A.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's Opposition contains citations and quotations that do not appear to exist; the court flagged these as false/nonexistent authorities. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: That Xiong v. Minga Wofford (2)
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Petitioner's brief cited 'G.A.A. v. Chestnut, No. 1:25-cv-01102-EPG, 2025 WL 3030589 (E.D. Cal. Nov. 21, 2025)'; Court found the Westlaw citation corresponded to a different real case, indicating a hallucinated citation likely from GenAI. Outcome: No Sanctions in circumstances. Order to Show Cause is here. The court concluded:"After careful consideration, including the involvement of a law student at the beginning of their legal career and for the reasons stated above, the Court exercises its discretion to discharge the OSC (ECF No. 49) and declines to impose sanctions on Petitioner’s counsel Cummings or law student [XX] for counsel’s failure to fully comply with the Court’s January 13, 2026 order. The Court repeats its admonishment that while AI and generative AI tools can assist counsel and parties, these tools must be used responsibly. They should not be used to verify legal authorityand citations, especially when generative AI tools were used to generate or identify the legal authority and citations being checked."

cases.name: Oscar Danilo Montealto Garcia v. Todd Lyons, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Petition cites Sanchez-Perez v. Garland, 2023 WL 5663021, at *3 (C.D. Cal. Aug. 29, 2023), which the court indicates appears nonexistent.

cases.name: Joseph Guy v. AFGE
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Motion asserts a case-based proposition phrased as a quotation that the Court found does not exist in the cited authority. Outcome: Order to Explain. Counsel's subsequent explanation can be found here.

cases.name: Pyle v. Glenn County Sheriff's Department
cases.summary: Fabricated: Other | The SAC contains a reference to 'Sheriff Owens' who is not named elsewhere in the pleadings; the court identified this as likely a GenAI-hallucinated factual insertion. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Song Dow Lee, et al. v. HSBC Bank USA, National Association, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiffs included a non-existent quotation and citation presented as from 'BPi Bright Power, Inc. v. Umpqua Holding Corp., 669 F.Supp.3d 904, 906 (N.D. Cal. 2023).' The court noted the citation/quote did not exist. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Gregoire v. Board of Trustees of SF BART
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Court was unable to locate three of the four cases cited in Plaintiff's opposition and withheld the case names to avoid amplifying possible errors; suggested these may be AI-fabricated authorities. Outcome: Monetary Sanction; Serve Order on client and Bar; CLE. Order to Show Cause is here. Counsel failed to answer.

cases.name: In re W.B.
cases.summary: Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Anthony C. Hill v. Workday, Inc. (2)
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Filed brief included a citation to a nonexistent case; court found the citation fabricated and tied its inclusion in part to AI use. Outcome: Admonishment; Monetary Fine; 4 hours live CLE; Order Circulation.

cases.name: Louis L. Ligon III v. Meta Platforms, Inc.
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Case Law Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Tekoma Chaney v. Transdev Services Inc. et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Attributed 'In re Shubert, 799 F.3d 1124, 1129 (9th Cir. 2015)' as authority in Plaintiff's Opposition to Ogilvie's MSJ (p.17); Court found the case fabricated and the reporter/pincite corresponds to an unrelated real case. Outcome: Monetary Sanction; Reporting to other courts.

cases.name: O.K., LLC v. Melka
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Respondent's brief asserts several cases cited by Melka do not exist; court noted respondent's claim as indicative of likely AI-generated (fabricated) citations but did not identify specific citations in the opinion.

cases.name: In re the Marriage of Joanne Rodrigues and Nathan Craig
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant's brief cited this case as authority; the court identified it as fictitious/nonexistent.

cases.name: Krista C. Geddes v. LoanCare, LLC, et al.
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Court found plaintiff misquoted and enlarged the scope of Kachlon; the quoted language does not appear in the opinion and was used to support removing nominal status for trustees. Outcome: Monetary Sanction; Bar Referral.

cases.name: Robert Harris v. Elementis Specialties, Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Defendant's filing included a completely hallucinated (fabricated) case citation generated by AI; court noted existence of a fabricated cite but the specific citation string was not provided in the opinion. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Michael T-Alexander v. Michelle Baass et al.
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Appellant included purported quotations attributed to Tennessee v. Lane that do not appear in that opinion; court found the quoted language fabricated. Outcome: Admonishment.

cases.name: Shaw v. City of Portola, et al.
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff used an incorrect pinpoint citation to People v. Dang, citing page 1305 though the published opinion ends at page 1300, and relied on Dang for a privilege proposition that is inapplicable; court identified the pin‑cite and inapposite reliance. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Zand v. Sukumar
cases.summary: Fabricated: Exhibits & Submissions

cases.name: Thanh Nguy v. Jabil Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Original opposition included a repeatedly-cited, purported E.D. Cal. decision that the court found likely to be a fictitious AI-generated case; counsel later filed a corrected brief removing the citation and the court declined to sanction. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Gamez v. County of Fresno
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Brief cites a case the Court could not locate; appears to be a nonexistent citation. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: James Jordan v. USA, Clinicas Del Camino Real, Inc., et al.
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff attributes a direct quotation to City of Chicago v. International College of Surgeons (522 U.S. 156) that does not appear in that opinion; plaintiff also mis-cited the case name order. Outcome: Admonishment.

cases.name: Modern Floor Specialists, Inc. et al. v. City of Los Angeles et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited a purported Ninth Circuit case 'Mabee v. County of Orange, 784 F. App'x 585, 588 (9th Cir. 2019)'; the court found no such case and that the reporter/pincite instead corresponds to Cochran v. City of Wichita (10th Cir.). Court ordered counsel to produce the Mabee opinion or show cause. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Najafpir v. Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited multiple non-existent cases in the TAC which the Court concluded were hallucinated by AI and held non-existent cases cannot support his asserted legal propositions. Outcome: Claim dismissed with prejudice.

cases.name: Saqib Kafeel v. Apple Inc., et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Reply cites a non-existent Ninth Circuit case; court notes the citation does not exist. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Leonard Colbert v. County of Riverside
cases.summary: Fabricated: Legal Norm | Opposition included a quoted purported provision of Cal. Gov't Code § 845.4 that the court could not locate in the statute or in any case; court suggested counsel may have used AI and failed to confirm accuracy. Outcome: Admonishment.

cases.name: Jared Ashcraft v. First-Citizens Bank and Trust Company, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited "GN Netcom, Inc. v. Plantronics, Inc., No. 12-cv-1247, 2016 WL 3635771, at *4–5 (D. Del. June 29, 2016)" but the Court's Westlaw search returned no documents for 2016 WL 3635771; the District of Delaware docket numbers and dates did not match and no opinion on that date was found. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Anna Sheerer v. Thomas Panas
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Respondent cited a non-existent case as authority; court identified the citation as fabricated. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Meriland Keith Dillard v. CBS Studios, Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Jorgensen v. CBS, Inc., 728 F.2d 958 (8th Cir. 1984),' which the Court found does not exist and misrepresents the reporter citation (728 F.2d 953 is United States v. Johns (7th Cir. 1984)). Court treated the citation as a fabricated authority. Outcome: Factor in dismissing with prejudice.

cases.name: Arno Kuigoua v. Adam Michael Sacks
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Second apparently nonexistent or miscited case citation in appellant's opening brief identified by respondent and noted by the court. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Joan Pablo Torres Campos v. Leslie Ann Munoz
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Court and counsel cited 'Marriage of Twigg' as authority on prioritizing parties' emotional well‑being in custody; Twigg does not exist and the citation "34 Cal.3d 926" and parallel citations provided by counsel were fabricated. Court found reliance on this nonexistent authority and counsel later admitted Twigg was fictitious. Outcome: Monetary sanction; Bar referral.

cases.name: In re Lusine Hakhverdyan
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Court observed Debtor cited 'Whittaker v. Otto, 2014 WL 37845 (9th Cir.)' which does not correspond to the authority described; the citation leads to an unrelated case (Harmer) and is inaccurate/nonexistent as cited.

cases.name: Estate of Khallid Muhammad et al v. Tupac Shakur Estate et al
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiffs quoted a Minnesota probate appeal as having "denied intervention where movants lacked legal title," but the underlying state decision did not address intervention; Court found the quotation/mischaracterization inaccurate. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Samuel K. v. Winsley Focia
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Opening brief cited a nonexistent case 'Medical Board v. Superior Court (2022) 88 Cal.App.5th 459, 475' and attributed multiple due-process quotations to it; court found no such published case or quotations and labeled them AI 'hallucinations.' Outcome: Appeal dismissed; appellant to bear her own costs on appeal..

cases.name: Anthony Jama Hall v. Superior Court of Sacramento County
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant cited 'County of Sacramento v. Superior Court (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 305' as controlling authority; the court determined this case citation does not exist and does not support appellant's proposition. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Juan Villalovos-Gutierrez, et al. v. Gerard Van de Pol, et al. (2)
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Defendant cited this case in its opposition; the court found the cited case does not exist. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Zeus Realty Group LLC v. 1032 N Sycamore Owner LA, LLC et al
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's Motion contained citations to nonexistent cases; Court noted the citations were nonexistent per Defendant's Opposition but did not identify specific fabricated cases.

cases.name: Bixler v. Church of Scientology
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | At least ten purported case authorities that have been miscited or mischaracterized in more than fifteen places in respondents’ brief. Outcome: Order to show cause.

cases.name: EFD USA, INC., et al. v. Band Pro Film and Digital, Inc., et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant quoted Lilienthal as stating tests for distinct claims ('(1) times of services performed; (2) specific obligations involved; and (3) resulting damages'), but the quotation does not appear in Lilienthal and the case does not address settlement offsets. Outcome: Monetary Sanction.

cases.name: Alejandro Rios v. Puente Hills Ford
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Rios misquoted language from ten existing cases; the court found the quoted language appears nowhere in those decisions (including purported quotations from Osumi v. Sutton (2007) and Fiore v. Alvord (1985)). Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Greenberg v. First Bank, Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiffs' counsel cited a non-existent, AI-generated case to support a legal proposition about CD surrender. Outcome: Upcoming Sanction.

cases.name: In re: Social Media Adolescent Addiction Litigation
cases.summary: Fabricated: Doctrinal Work | Deposition admission that some academic articles cited by Osborne do not exist; defendants attribute those citations to AI-generated fabrications; court noted plaintiffs said citations were corrected and declined to exclude the expert on that basis. Outcome: Court declined to exclude the expert based on the AI-generated/incorrect citations; issue reserved for cross-examination; motion to exclude denied.. Defendants contended that Dr. Brian Osborne relied on nonexistent academic articles and miscited sources generated by an AI citation tool. Plaintiffs said the errors were formatting miscites from an AI citation tool and were corrected. The Court declined to exclude Osborne's opinions on this basis, permitting defendants to explore the issue on cross-examination and barring any regurgitation of hearsay at trial.

cases.name: Creditors Adjustment Bureau, Inc. v. All Season Power LLC, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's counsel cited a non-existent (phantom) case in its brief; counsel later admitted the citation was from a non-existent case produced by AI-assisted research and the court noted the error. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: TQJ, LLC v. Jennifer Esquivel et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Citation pointed to an unrelated case (United States v. De La Paz) and the purported Muller decision cited (43 F. Supp. 2d 372, 379 (S.D.N.Y. 1999)) could not be located as cited; an older unrelated Muller decision exists but does not support the proposition. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Daniel James Cummins v. Moises Becerra
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Petitioner's reply included a citation to 'Vuong' which, per petitioner's errata and the court, does not appear in publicly available legal databases and was identified as erroneous. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Sebastian Rako v. VMware LLC (2)
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff (pro se) previously cited non-existent/fictitious cases in filings; VMware asserted those citations were likely AI-hallucinated and the Court warned against filing such fabricated case citations. Outcome: Required meet-and-confer for AI-use disputes.

cases.name: Ava Naeini v. Confluent Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant cited 'Green v. Casa Santa Fe (2001) 94 Cal.App.4th 638'; the court found the citation is not valid and was unable to locate the purported authority.

cases.name: MacroCharts Research LLC v. Tony Chou
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Chou's opposition contained nonexistent legal authorities; Plaintiff identified the 'nonexistent' or 'hallucinated' citations and Chou subsequently filed an errata removing them. The Court called the citations 'nonexistent' but declined sanctions. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: That Xiong v. Minga Wofford (1)
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Petitioner's reply cited 'Phan v. Barr, No. 1:19-cv-01451, 2019 WL 7758773 (E.D. Cal. Dec. 20, 2019)', which the Court identified as an incorrect/nonexistent citation; petitioner later admitted error and submitted a different, real 'Phan' citation. Outcome: Order to inform client and staff of errors.

cases.name: Landberg v City of New York
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Brief cited a nonexistent New York case for the standard of appellate review of summary judgment. Outcome: Monetary Sanction.

cases.name: Jason Wimberly v. Verizon New York Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited a non-existent opinion (United States v. Chawla, 453 F. Supp. 3d 571 (E.D.N.Y. 2020)); the Court could not locate the case and found the citation instead led to United States v. Bedi, which did not support the cited proposition. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: LiveVideo.AI Corp. v. Redstone
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Struck motions contained hallucinated, non-existent case citations asserted as authority; court found no real authority supporting the arguments. Outcome: Adverse Costs Order; Bar Referral.

cases.name: Owens v. Select Portfolio Servicing, Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Court observed two citations were not to decisions at all (i.e., fabricated or non-existent authorities); defendant pointed this out. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: In the Matter of Kevin A. Julien v. Danielle M. Arthur
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellant's brief cited a nonexistent Appellate Division, First Department decision to support a claim of judicial bias; court treated the citation as fabricated and linked it to unverified GenAI/online research use. Outcome: Monetary Sanction.

cases.name: Polanco v. Himalayan Real Estate LLC, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited a nonexistent U.S. Supreme Court opinion, "Moore v. Alliance Credit Union," claiming it held foreclosures based on fiat currency unconstitutional; the Court found the opinion fictitious and warned against citing fake authorities. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Hodges v. McGough Enterprises LLC
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Court was unable to locate or verify this case citation cited by Plaintiff and raised concern it may be AI-generated; Plaintiff directed to produce a copy. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Sana Hazina Butler v. Fidelity Management & Research Company LLC
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Rate Technology Inc. v. Speakeasy Inc.' with an incorrect citation and relied on it for a point; the actual case (Rates Tech. Inc. v. Speakeasy, Inc., 685 F.3d 163 (2d Cir. 2012)) concerns a settlement agreement and patent validity, not the cited proposition. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Landberg v City of New York
cases.summary: Outcome: Order to Show Cause. Subsequent oral argument discussed the incident, see here.

cases.name: Chakma v. Sushi Katsuei, Inc.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Doctrinal Work | Defense Counsel cited a non-sourced authority "§ 3.01 Certification Is Crucial Step In Class Actions," which the Court found resulted from AI hallucination and lacked any identifiable source. Outcome: Monetary Sanction.

cases.name: Waggeh v. Utility Workers Union of America Local 1-2 et al.
cases.summary: Outcome: Public Admonishment.

cases.name: In the Matter of Janelle Melissa Lewis, an attorney and counselor-at-law
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Respondent's draft brief contained numerous citations to non-existent cases; the U.S. District Court characterized those citations as hallucinations produced by ChatGPT or a similar AI. Outcome: Monetary Sanction; Bar Suspension.

cases.name: Henri Giovani Morales Jimenez v. Christopher Shanahan
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel relied on at least one nonexistent case; the court found that at least one cited authority did not exist. Outcome: Admonishment.

cases.name: Terrance Anthony Norris v. JPMorgan Chase Bank N.A.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cites a non-existent case, GAI Consultants, Inc. v. Homestead Vill. Ltd. P’ship, 961 F. Supp. 1437 (E.D. Pa. 1997); Court notes the opinion does not exist. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Mercy Wellington v. City of New York
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's initial opposition cited six purported authorities that do not appear in legal databases and which the Court concluded were likely AI-hallucinated non-existent opinions; Court could not locate them and warned plaintiff. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Sana Hazina Butler v. Fidelity Management & Research Company LLC
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Maersk, Inc. v. Neewra, Inc. with quoted language the court determined does not appear in that opinion; case exists but does not support the cited proposition. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Superb Motors Inc. et al. v. Anthony Deo et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Opposing party identified 'In re Amica Mut. Ins. Co., 85 A.D.3d 1510 (3rd Dept. 2011)' as a nonexistent case; court treated it as an AI-generated fabricated citation. Outcome: Monetary Sanction. In his Response to the Order to Show Cause (available here), Counsel blamed the incident on virus and malwares allegedly impacting his computer.

cases.name: Sai Malena Jimenez-Fogarty v. Thomas Fogarty et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Counsel cited a Westlaw/database identifier and party name that do not match any opinion on the cited topic; the citation corresponded to an unrelated docket or jurisdiction. Outcome: Monetary Sanction; Order to Notice Client.

cases.name: In the Matter of the Application of Jonathan David Rinaldi
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Petitioner cited nonexistent case law in his memorandum; court observed some citations appeared fabricated.

cases.name: Rhonda L. H.-B. v. Commissioner of Social Security
cases.summary: Fabricated: Exhibits & Submissions | Multiple templated captions and placeholders (e.g., venue/case fields left as blanks or bracketed placeholders) indicating unedited AI-generated output. Outcome: Warning; Refiling Allowed.

cases.name: In re Prince Global Holdings Limited, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | AI-generated, non-existent/incorrect Westlaw citation for Three Arrows Capital appearing in the Motion; corrected in Schedule A. Outcome: Firm apologised. Sullivan & Cromwell acknowledged that its April 9, 2026 Motion contained AI-generated inaccuracies including fabricated and misquoted case citations. The firm apologized, identified and corrected the errors in Schedule A, undertook remedial measures, and filed corrected papers. The issue was brought to the firm's attention by opposing counsel (Boies Schiller Flexner).

cases.name: Viral Bhagat v. Anuja Sharad Shah
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Defendant cited "In re SunEdison, Inc. Sec. Litig., 314 F.R.D. 139, 143 (S.D.N.Y. 2016)"; the court could not locate the case or quoted text on docket or Westlaw and concluded the citation was an AI-generated fabrication. Outcome: Admonishment.

cases.name: Evgeny Yaroshevsky v. Karlin et al.
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Other | Plaintiff admitted using Google Gemini to obtain citations and failed to verify them before filing, contributing to the submission of fabricated and inaccurate authorities. Outcome: Recommended adverse costs order. R&R was adopted later on (see here).

cases.name: 76 Route 6 Holdings Inc. v. Town of Yorktown, NY
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiff claimed McEachin v. McGuinnis contains language applying 'special solicitude' to civil rights plaintiffs; the Court determined McEachin contains no such language and admonished counsel. Outcome: Admonishment.

cases.name: Beedemariam Kassaw v. Wal-Mart Corporation
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's January 2026 memorandum/sur-reply (Dkt. 79) included AI-generated 'hallucinated cases'—fabricated case citations; Court found apparent use of AI and ordered Dkt. 79 stricken. Outcome: Brief Struck.

cases.name: Andrea K. Tantaros v. Fox News Network, LLC, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Roe v. Bernabei & Katz, PLLC, 314 F. Supp. 3d 200,209 (D.D.C. 2018)' as analogous authority; Court identified this citation as inaccurate/non-existent. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Fecteau v. Safety National Casualty Corporation
cases.summary: Fabricated: Legal Norm | Plaintiff quoted non-existent statutory language as if from 50 U.S.C. § 3936, asserting it tolled filing deadlines (the Court found the quoted language does not appear in § 3936).

cases.name: Donte McClellon v. E. Rickard, Warden of FCI Otisville, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Petitioner’s reply cited at least three judicial opinions that do not exist; Magistrate Judge Moses identified these as likely AI-generated fabricated cases. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Cartagena v. Dixon, Blackburn, and T.A. Blackburn Law, PLLC
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Brief filed by defendant's counsel contained multiple non-existent case citations and misstatements of law; court found pervasive 'hallucinated' cases and noted counsel attributed errors to use of 'Protégé' AI. Outcome: Admonishment.

cases.name: M7 Indústria e Comércio de Compensados e Laminados v. U.S. Structural Plywood Integrity Coalition, et al.
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law | Plaintiffs' memorandum attributed this quoted passage to NYNEX Corp. v. Discon, but the Court found the quotation does not appear in that opinion. Outcome: Order to show cause. The Court found two quotations in the Plaintiffs' memorandum that do not appear in the cited federal opinions and ordered counsel to explain and certify review of all cited cases before deciding whether to impose further sanctions.

cases.name: Jeri'yah Ford v. Troy City School District, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's Response cited unspecified Eighth Circuit and D. Kan. cases that opposing counsel and the Court could not locate; court concluded these were fabricated and ordered show cause. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: H.F. v. State
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Four Court of Claims cases cited in the Manning Affirmation could not be located and were treated as fictitious by the court. Outcome: Brief Partly Struck; Warning.

cases.name: Matter of Zareh
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Brief contained numerous citation errors, including internally inconsistent and unverifiable citations; District Court concluded some citations were AI-generated and could not be verified. Outcome: Respondent publicly censured. Respondent filed an opposition brief in federal litigation containing numerous citation errors and misstatements of law. The District Court found the brief AI-generated, noting ChatGPT described at least one cited case in the same erroneous manner, and admonished counsel; this Court imposed reciprocal discipline in the form of a public censure for failure to supervise and for filing an unreviewed AI-drafted brief.

cases.name: Ave. Capital Group, LLC v. Strum
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Court found counsel included a non-existent case citation in his memorandum and ordered show-cause re sanctions. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Flycatcher v. Affable Avenue
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Affable's motion to dismiss included numerous non-existent case citations generated by AI; Opposing counsel identified at least 13 fabricated cases and the Court found 14 of 60 citations to be fake. Outcome: Brief Struck; Default Judgment. Counsel Steven A. Feldman repeatedly filed submissions containing AI-generated nonexistent case citations and misattributed quotations. Opposing counsel flagged numerous errors; the Court found fabricated citations and false quotes, concluded counsel acted in bad faith or with conscious avoidance, struck the filings, and entered default judgment against counsel's client Affable Avenue LLC. The Court permitted opposing counsel to apply for attorneys' fees.

cases.name: 1S REO Opportunity 1, LLC v. 223 Howard LLC
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited Gustavia Home, LLC v. Rice, 2020 WL 4917915 (E.D.N.Y. Aug. 21, 2020), a Westlaw citation that does not exist; the number corresponds to Durrett v. IKO Indus., Inc. (Ky. Ct. App.), and the actual Gustavia Rice decision cited by court is 2016 WL 6683473 which states the opposite proposition.

cases.name: Cassata v. Michael Macrina Architect, P.C.
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Case Law | Counsel cited Becker v Elm Air Conditioning Corp., 143 AD2d 965, 966 (2nd Dep't 1988) for a proposition the case does not support; court found mischaracterization of the holding. Outcome: Monetary Sanction; Brief struck.

cases.name: Avalon Holdings Corp. v. Guy Gentile
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Gentile cited a non-existent Second Circuit opinion and attributed a quotation to it; the court could not locate any such opinion or the quoted language and concluded the citation was fictitious, possibly produced by generative AI. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Ava Naeini v. Confluent Inc.
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Case Law | Plaintiff cited "Matter of Polizzi v Polizzi, 226 AD2d 581 (2d Dept 1996)"; court found the citation actually leads to Epes v Healey and does not support the proposition asserted.

cases.name: Corst v. Mushailov
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Citation “Matter of Polizzi v Polizzi, 226 AD2d 581 (2d Dept 1996)” did not correspond to that matter and led to a different case (Epes v Healey); court found it defective/unverified. Outcome: Apology from Counsel.

cases.name: Wurtenberg v. The City of New York
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's papers relied on non-existent legal authority (fabricated cases); court found these authorities did not exist and attributed them to misuse of generative AI. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Deutsche Bank National Bank v. Jean LeTennier
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Submission of no less than 23 fabricated legal authorities across five filings, identified by plaintiff and confirmed by the court. Outcome: Orders affirmed; monetary sanctions imposed (counsel $5,000; defendant $2,500) and costs awarded.

cases.name: Burnett v. The City of New York
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff's counsel cited a nonexistent case (Backus v. City of Rochester, 148 AD3d 1697 [4th Dept 2017]); the court reported it was unable to locate the case and ordered counsel to show cause why he should not be sanctioned. Outcome: Order to Show Cause.

cases.name: Labatt USA Operating v. Friends Beverage Group
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Court found defendants' opposition and cross-motion contained hallucinated/fabricated case citations generated by AI; filings were stricken. Outcome: Filings struck. By interim order dated March 17, 2025 the court struck defendants' opposition and cross-motion after finding the filings, drafted with AI, contained hallucinated (fabricated) case citations. The court treated the motion as unopposed thereafter.

cases.name: Ng v. AmGuard Insurance Company, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited 'Greyhound-Thermalink, Inc. v. B.U.R. Realty Corp., 42 A.D.3d 319, 320 (N.Y. 1st Dep’t 2007)', which the Court's research found does not appear to exist; Court warned about AI-generated fake citations. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Smith v. Clarence Smith et al.

cases.name: Ezenwa Ebem v. Bondi et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Exhibits & Submissions | Plaintiff repeatedly asserted a 'Clerk's Entry of Default' though the Clerk never entered default; the filings were refiling/relabeling of a motion (Dkt. The court found the plaintiff's filings contained misrepresentations of the record—specifically, a purported 'Clerk's Entry of Default' that never existed and a claim that an Immigration Judge made a final binding APA finding. The court attributed these misrepresentations to likely AI generation, warned the plaintiff about consequences for false statements, and construed the misrepresentations as AI misapplication rather than deliberate deception.

The court warned the plaintiff and ordered him to provide copies of cited cases in future filings; recommended dismissal of federal claims and possible sanctions for continued misrepresentations.

Smith' (with a Texas bar number) but admitted she never met the person and the State Bar had no record. Outcome: Order to show cause. Outcome: Magistrate Judge recommended Rule 11 sanctions. Magistrate Judge found Plaintiff's Reply contained multiple citations that do not exist, quotes not found in the cited authorities, and material mischaracterizations of cases; court concluded counsel likely used generative AI and failed to verify outputs and recommended the District Court consider Rule 11 sanctions.

cases.name: USA v. Sethi

cases.name: In re Sonja Helvig DeRosa-Grund
cases.summary: False Quotes: Case Law Outcome: Case dismissed with prejudice for one year. The debtor, Sonja Helvig DeRosa-Grund, engaged in substantial abuse of the Chapter 13 process by filing numerous frivolous motions and making false allegations against opposing counsel. The debtor repeatedly cited non-existent case law and fabricated quotes from existing cases, despite being warned about this behavior. The court dismissed the case with prejudice for one year, terminated the debtor's ECF filing privileges, and imposed additional protective measures to prevent future abuse. The court found that the debtor violated Bankruptcy Rule 9011(b) by making arguments based on non-existent case law and misquoting cases.

cases.name: Karina Elizondo vs. City of Laredo
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff’s response included fictitious case citations; the Court found some citations were fabricated rather than real cases. Outcome: Monetary sanction; mandatory CLE in ethics/legal technology. Counsel admitted his law clerk used AI tools and failed to verify the citations. The court imposed a $2,500 monetary sanction, required completion of CLE in ethics/legal technology, and ordered service of the sanction order to the plaintiff.

cases.name: In re Cao

App. P. 52.11(a) (“On motion of any party or on its own initiative, the court may--after notice and a reasonable opportunity to respond--impose just sanctions on a party or attorney who is not acting in good faith as indicated by any of the following... filing a petition that is clearly groundless.”) "

cases.name: Leafwell, Inc. v. The Doc App, Inc. (2)
cases.summary: Misrepresented: Case Law | Castro overstated the breadth or holdings of four cases, presenting authorities as supporting broader propositions than they did. Because existing sanctions from a prior order remained and the court found additional sanctions excessive, it denied the plaintiff's motion for further sanctions. The court attributed much of the problem to Castro's failure to review AI-generated material rather than prohibiting AI use outright.

cases.name: Ayatollah Hylton v. Chivone Janee Hylton, et al.
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Plaintiff cited and quoted a non-existent order: attributed the language "A last-minute email or single communication is insufficient to satisfy Local Rule 7.1(a)(3). cases.name: Friend v. Serpa
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellee included multiple non-existent case citations (phantom authority); court found them fabricated and instructed they be disregarded. Outcome: Warning.

cases.name: Russell v. Mells
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellee's brief cited a nonexistent case, 'Cade v. Roberts, 403 So. 2d 516 (Fla. 5th DCA 1981)'; court found no reported case by that name and that the cited reporter page pertained to an unrelated opinion (Girardeau v. State). Outcome: Bar Referral. "Unfortunately, we're finding this problem arising more and more frequently […] When a lawyer cites imaginary legal authorities to our court as if they were law, we are compelled to refer that lawyer to the Bar because of the professional rules of conduct. It doesn't take much moral imagination to understand why. As judges, we rely on attorneys to ethically represent their clients. We expect that representation to be zealous, honest, and competent. Indeed, lawyers owe the courts and their clients a duty to practice with competence and candor. By signing an appellate brief, a lawyer certifies that he or she has read the document and that to the best of the lawyer's knowledge, information, and belief there are "good grounds to support the document."These ethical requirements are not excused simply because a computer program generated a faulty or misleading legal analysis. Nor is it an excuse that the attorney did not intend to mislead the court. "To state the obvious, it is a fundamental duty of attorneys to read the legal authorities they cite in appellate briefs or any other court filings to determine that the authorities stand for the propositions for which they are cited."Obviously, that didn't happen when Ms. McLane filed this answer brief. Instead, counsel "fundamentally abdicated" her duty to the court and her client when she submitted this filing without verifying that the three cases cited in her brief said what she claimed they said. Accordingly, it is our duty to refer this matter to the Florida Bar to proceed as it deems appropriate."

cases.name: Russell v. Mells
cases.summary: Fabricated: Case Law | Appellee's brief cited a non-existent authority, "Cade v. Roberts, 403 So. 2d 516 (Fla. 5th DCA 1981)," as support for a motion-to-dismiss principle; court's research found no such reported case and that the cited page belonged to Girardeau v. State, which is unrelated. Outcome: Order to Show Cause; Bar Referral.
